parsedContent.title: School Bursar & Accounting — Practice Exam
parsedContent.description: 250 questions · Multiple choice · Click 'Show explanation' to reveal the worked answer

sections.name: A
sections.title: Section A — Accounting Fundamentals & Double Entry (Q1–10)

questions.text: Q1 Which of the following best describes the purpose of a ledger account?

options.text: A. To list all bank transactions chronologically
B. To record all transactions relating to a specific item, person or expense
C. To summarise the annual income and expenditure
D. To show the bank balance at a given date

questions.text: Q2 The dual concept in accounting means that:

options.text: A. Accounts must be prepared twice a year
B. Every transaction affects two accounts — one debit and one credit
C. The school keeps two sets of books
D. Assets must equal twice the liabilities

questions.text: Q3 A school paid wages of FRW 850,000 in cash. The correct journal entry is:

options.text: A. Dr Wages A/C, Cr Cash A/C
B. Dr Cash A/C, Cr Wages A/C
C. Dr Wages A/C, Cr Capital A/C
D. Dr Expenses A/C, Cr Bank A/C

questions.text: Q4 Which of the following is a book of original entry?
Q5 The accounting equation can be correctly expressed as:
Q6 A school sells old desks on credit for FRW 320,000. Which entry is correct?

options.text: A. Dr Cash A/C, Cr Sales A/C
B. Dr Debtors A/C, Cr Sales A/C
C. Dr Sales A/C, Cr Debtors A/C
D. Dr Bank A/C, Cr Furniture A/C

questions.text: Q7 Which accounting concept states that the business is separate from its owner?
Q8 A narration in the journal is:

options.text: A. The date of the transaction
B. A brief explanation written below the journal entry
C. The reference number of the source document
D. The name of the person who authorised the transaction

questions.text: Q9 Which of the following is NOT a source document?
Q10 When a school receives cash from a debtor, the correct entry is:

options.text: A. Dr Debtors A/C, Cr Cash A/C
B. Dr Cash A/C, Cr Debtors A/C
C. Dr Cash A/C, Cr Capital A/C
D. Dr Sales A/C, Cr Cash A/C

sections.name: B
sections.title: Section B — Cash Book, Bank Reconciliation & Petty Cash (Q11–20)

questions.text: Q11 A school bursar gave a petty cash float of FRW 300,000. By the end of the week, FRW 214,500 had been spent. How much is reimbursed to restore the imprest?
Q12 Bank charges of FRW 22,000 appear on the bank statement but have not been entered in the cash book. To update the cash book, you should:

options.text: A. Add FRW 22,000 to the cash book balance
B. Deduct FRW 22,000 from the cash book balance
C. Add FRW 22,000 to the bank statement balance
D. Leave the cash book unchanged

questions.text: Q13 Unpresented cheques in a bank reconciliation refer to:

options.text: A. Cheques received but not yet deposited
B. Cheques that have been cancelled
C. Cheques issued by the school but not yet cleared by the bank
D. Cheques returned due to insufficient funds

questions.text: Q14 A school's cash book shows a bank balance of FRW 5,600,000. The bank statement shows FRW 6,100,000. Unpresented cheques total FRW 700,000 and uncredited deposits total FRW 200,000. Which of the following explains the difference?

options.text: A. The bank statement is wrong
B. Unpresented cheques cause the bank to show a higher balance
C. Uncredited deposits cause the cash book to show a higher balance

questions.text: Q15 Interest earned of FRW 35,000 is credited by the bank but not yet entered in the cash book. The bank reconciliation adjustment is to:

options.text: A. Deduct FRW 35,000 from the bank statement
B. Add FRW 35,000 to the cash book balance
C. Deduct FRW 35,000 from the cash book

questions.text: Q16 A cheque for FRW 180,000 received from a parent and deposited by the school was returned by the bank marked 'NSF'. The correction entry is:

options.text: A. Dr Bank A/C, Cr Parent A/C
B. Dr Parent (Debtor) A/C, Cr Bank A/C
C. Dr Cash A/C, Cr Bank A/C
D. Dr Bank A/C, Cr Cash A/C

questions.text: Q17 Which of the following will NOT appear in a bank reconciliation statement?

options.text: D. Direct bank transfers not in cash book

questions.text: Q18 A standing order payment of FRW 95,000 appears on the bank statement but not in the cash book. The adjusted cash book balance should be:
Q19 The bank statement of a school shows a credit balance. This means the school:

options.text: B. Has money in the bank

questions.text: Q20 A school's cash book (bank column) shows a balance of FRW 4,200,000. Bank statement balance is FRW 3,850,000. Outstanding deposits total FRW 600,000. What are the unpresented cheques?

sections.name: C
sections.title: Section C — Ledgers, Trial Balance & Errors (Q21–30)

questions.text: Q21 Which of the following has a normal credit balance?
Q22 The drawings ledger account has a normal:

options.text: D. Variable balance depending on the period

questions.text: Q23 A trial balance that agrees proves:

options.text: A. That all transactions have been recorded correctly
B. That there are no errors in the books
C. That total debits equal total credits
D. That the financial statements are accurate

questions.text: Q24 A school entered a cash sale of FRW 150,000 in the debtors ledger instead of the cash book. This is an error of:
Q25 An electricity bill of FRW 78,000 was completely omitted from the books. This is an error of:
Q26 A purchase of FRW 56,000 was recorded as FRW 65,000 in the purchases day book. This is an error of:
Q27 Which of the following errors will NOT be detected by a trial balance?

options.text: A. A debit entry with no corresponding credit
B. A transposition error in only one account
C. A purchase recorded as a sale in both accounts
D. An arithmetic error in one account balance

questions.text: Q28 When a trial balance fails to agree, the difference is placed in a:
Q29 The sales ledger (debtors ledger) contains accounts for:

options.text: B. Individual customers who owe the school money

questions.text: Q30 Closing stock appearing only in the trial balance (not yet in final accounts) is shown in the:

options.text: A. Trading account and balance sheet
C. Profit and loss account only

sections.name: D
sections.title: Section D — Depreciation, Capital & Revenue Expenditure (Q31–38)

questions.text: Q31 A school bought science laboratory equipment for FRW 9,000,000 on 1 January. Annual depreciation is 20% straight line. What is the net book value at the end of Year 2?
Q32 A motor vehicle costing FRW 15,000,000 has a residual value of FRW 3,000,000 and a useful life of 4 years. Annual straight-line depreciation is:
Q33 A school purchased a photocopier for $12,000 and depreciated it at 25% reducing balance. Tax rate is 30%. Calculate the depreciation tax shield in Year 2.
Q34 Which of the following is classified as revenue expenditure?

options.text: A. Purchase of a school bus
C. Annual servicing of school vehicles
D. Legal fees to acquire land

questions.text: Q35 A school sold a computer with a net book value of FRW 1,200,000 for FRW 900,000. The result is:

options.text: A. Profit on disposal of FRW 300,000
B. Loss on disposal of FRW 300,000

questions.text: Q36 Goodwill arises when a school purchases another institution. It is calculated as:

options.text: A. Total assets minus total liabilities
B. Purchase price minus fair value of net assets acquired
C. Total income minus total expenditure

questions.text: Q37 The going concern concept affects depreciation because:

options.text: A. Assets must be revalued annually
B. Depreciation is only charged when assets are used
C. Assets are depreciated over their useful life assuming the business will continue operating
D. The historical cost of assets must increase each year

questions.text: Q38 A school paid FRW 500,000 to a lawyer to successfully defend a lawsuit over ownership of school land. This is:

sections.name: E
sections.title: Section E — Stock Control, Balance Sheet & Financial Statements (Q39–50)

questions.text: Q39 Maximum consumption is 8,000 units/week, minimum consumption is 5,000 units/week, and maximum lead time is 6 weeks. Calculate the re-order level.
Q40 Annual demand for school chalk is 24,000 boxes. Ordering cost is $60 per order and holding cost is $0.60 per box per year. Calculate the EOQ.
Q41 The minimum stock level formula is:

options.text: A. Re-order level − (Maximum consumption × Maximum lead time)
B. Re-order level − (Average consumption × Average lead time)
C. Re-order level + Re-order quantity − (Minimum consumption × Minimum lead time)

questions.text: Q42 A school's maximum stock level is 42,000 units, minimum stock level is 8,000 units. The average stock level is:
Q43 A school's balance sheet shows: Cash FRW 1,200,000, Debtors FRW 800,000, Stock FRW 500,000, Equipment FRW 6,000,000, Creditors FRW 1,100,000, Bank loan FRW 2,000,000. What is the capital?
Q44 Which of the following is NOT included in an income and expenditure account for a non-profit school?
Q45 A school's opening capital is FRW 38,000,000. During the year, the surplus was FRW 6,500,000 and drawings were FRW 2,000,000. What is the closing capital?
Q46 Accrued expenses (outstanding expenses) appear in the balance sheet as:
Q47 A school's opening stock is FRW 3,200,000, purchases are FRW 18,500,000, and closing stock is FRW 4,100,000. The cost of goods sold is:
Q48 Which of the following correctly describes a receipts and payments account?

options.text: A. A summary of all income earned and expenses incurred during the period
B. A record of all cash and bank receipts and payments during the period
C. A statement of the organisation's financial position
D. A list of all assets and liabilities

questions.text: Q49 The historical cost concept requires that assets be recorded at:
Q50 A school's gross profit is FRW 14,400,000 and net sales are FRW 36,000,000. The gross profit margin is:
